words.definitionEn: a formal examination of evidence in court by a judge and often a jury, to decide if somebody accused of a crime is guilty or not
words.exampleEn: a murder/criminal trial

words.definitionEn: a social group in a traditional society consisting of people with the same language, culture, religion, etc., living in a particular area and often having one leader known as a chief
words.exampleEn: They were a nomadic horse-riding tribe.

words.definitionEn: to make something happen suddenly
words.exampleEn: Nuts can trigger off a violent allergic reaction.

words.definitionEn: to catch your foot on something and fall or almost fall
words.exampleEn: She tripped and fell.

words.definitionEn: soldiers, especially in large groups
words.exampleEn: They announced the withdrawal of 12 000 troops from the area.

words.definitionEn: coming from, found in or typical of the tropics
words.exampleEn: tropical fish/birds/fruit

words.definitionEn: to make somebody worried or upset
words.exampleEn: What is it that's troubling you?

words.definitionEn: used to emphasize that a particular statement, feeling, etc. is sincere or real
words.exampleEn: She truly believes that none of this is her fault.

words.definitionEn: the belief that somebody/something is good, sincere, honest, etc. and will not try to harm or trick you
words.exampleEn: Our partnership is based on trust.

words.definitionEn: to have confidence in somebody; to believe that somebody is good, sincere, honest, etc.
words.exampleEn: trust somebody, She trusts Alan implicitly.

words.definitionEn: an act of trying to do something
words.exampleEn: I doubt they'll be able to help but it's worth a try (= worth asking them).

words.definitionEn: an extremely large wave in the sea caused, for example, by an earthquake
words.exampleEn: A tsunami early warning system was set up in Hawaii.

words.definitionEn: a series of musical notes that are sung or played in a particular order to form a piece of music
words.exampleEn: to sing/whistle a tune

words.definitionEn: a passage built underground, for example to allow a road or railway to go through a hill, under a river, etc.
words.exampleEn: a railway/railroad tunnel

words.definitionEn: happening at the end of a long process
words.exampleEn: our ultimate goal/aim/objective/target

words.definitionEn: in the end; finally
words.exampleEn: A poor diet will ultimately lead to illness.

words.definitionEn: so bad that you think it should not be allowed
words.exampleEn: Such behaviour is totally unacceptable in a civilized society.

words.definitionEn: the state of being uncertain
words.exampleEn: There is considerable uncertainty about the company's future.

words.definitionEn: in a state like sleep because of an injury or illness, and not able to use your senses
words.exampleEn: They found him lying unconscious on the floor.

words.definitionEn: to experience something, especially a change or something unpleasant
words.exampleEn: to undergo tests/trials/repairs

words.definitionEn: having a strong, unpleasant taste; not sweet
words.exampleEn: Black coffee leaves a bitter taste in the mouth.

words.definitionEn: to make yourself responsible for something and start doing it
words.exampleEn: to undertake a task/project

words.definitionEn: if something is unexpected, it surprises you because you were not expecting it
words.exampleEn: Things took an unexpected turn.

words.definitionEn: to spread open or flat something that has previously been folded; to become open and flat
words.exampleEn: to unfold a map

words.definitionEn: having bad luck; caused by bad luck
words.exampleEn: He was unfortunate to lose in the final round.

words.definitionEn: being the only one of its kind
words.exampleEn: Everyone's fingerprints are unique.

words.definitionEn: to join together with other people in order to do something as a group
words.exampleEn: Nationalist parties united to oppose the government's plans.

words.definitionEn: the state of being in agreement and working together; the state of being joined together to form one unit
words.exampleEn: European unity

words.definitionEn: done by or involving all the people in the world or in a particular group
words.exampleEn: Such problems are a universal feature of old age.

words.definitionEn: the whole of space and everything in it, including the earth, the planets and the stars
words.exampleEn: in the universe, Could there be intelligent life elsewhere in the universe?

words.definitionEn: not known or identified
words.exampleEn: A previously unknown group claimed responsibility for the bombing.

words.definitionEn: located above something else, especially something of the same type or the other of a pair
words.exampleEn: her upper lip

words.definitionEn: responsibility for doing something badly or wrongly; saying that somebody is responsible for something
words.exampleEn: Why do I always get the blame for everything that goes wrong?

words.definitionEn: towards a higher place or position
words.exampleEn: Place your hands on the table with the palms facing upwards.

words.definitionEn: connected with a town or city
words.exampleEn: urban and rural communities

words.definitionEn: to advise or try hard to persuade somebody to do something
words.exampleEn: urge somebody to do something, If you ever get the chance to visit this place, I strongly urge you to do so.

words.definitionEn: that needs to be dealt with or happen immediately
words.exampleEn: The police have issued an urgent appeal for information.

words.definitionEn: the way in which words are used in a language
words.exampleEn: a book on current English usage

words.definitionEn: to think or say that somebody/something is responsible for something bad
words.exampleEn: blame somebody/something for something, She doesn't blame anyone for her father's death.

words.definitionEn: not useful; not doing or achieving what is needed or wanted
words.exampleEn: This pen is useless.

words.definitionEn: that is legally or officially acceptable
words.exampleEn: Do you have a valid passport?

words.definitionEn: to think that somebody/something is important
words.exampleEn: value somebody/something, He has come to value her advice and support.

words.definitionEn: a change, especially in the amount or level of something
words.exampleEn: The dial records very slight variations in pressure.

words.definitionEn: to be different from each other in size, shape, etc.
words.exampleEn: New techniques were introduced with varying degrees of success.

words.definitionEn: extremely large in area, size, amount, etc.
words.exampleEn: a vast area of forest

words.definitionEn: a large cover, often made of wool, used especially on beds to keep people warm
words.exampleEn: It’s cold tonight—can I have another blanket?

words.definitionEn: a place where people meet for an organized event, for example a concert, sporting event or conference
words.exampleEn: The band will be playing at 20 different venues on their UK tour.

words.definitionEn: going straight up or down from a level surface or from top to bottom in a picture, etc.
words.exampleEn: the vertical axis of the graph

words.definitionEn: used to emphasize that you are talking about a particular thing or person and not about another
words.exampleEn: He might be phoning her at this very moment.

words.definitionEn: through a place
words.exampleEn: We flew home via Dubai.

words.definitionEn: success in a game, an election, a war, etc.
words.exampleEn: to win a narrow victory

words.definitionEn: a way of thinking about a subject
words.exampleEn: from a… viewpoint, Try looking at things from a different viewpoint.

words.definitionEn: violent behaviour that is intended to hurt or kill somebody
words.exampleEn: Police do not think this killing was a random act of violence.

words.definitionEn: made to appear to exist by the use of computer software, for example on the internet
words.exampleEn: a system to help programmers create virtual environments

words.definitionEn: a stamp or mark put in your passport by officials of a foreign country that gives you permission to enter, pass through or leave their country
words.exampleEn: to apply for a visa

words.definitionEn: that can be seen
words.exampleEn: The house is clearly visible from the beach.

words.definitionEn: the ability to see; the area that you can see from a particular position
words.exampleEn: to have good/perfect/poor/blurred/normal vision

words.definitionEn: of or connected with seeing or sight
words.exampleEn: the visual arts

words.definitionEn: necessary or essential in order for something to succeed or exist
words.exampleEn: vital for somebody/something, the vitamins that are vital for health

words.definitionEn: a natural substance found in food that is an essential part of what humans and animals need to help them grow and stay healthy. There are many different vitamins.
words.exampleEn: vitamin A/B/D/E

words.definitionEn: the amount of space that an object or a substance fills; the amount of space that a container has
words.exampleEn: volume of something, How do you measure the volume of a gas?

words.definitionEn: done willingly, not because you are forced
words.exampleEn: a voluntary agreement

words.definitionEn: the action of choosing somebody/something in an election or at a meeting
words.exampleEn: He was eliminated in the first round of voting.

words.definitionEn: a regular amount of money that you earn, usually every week or every month, for work or services
words.exampleEn: wage/wages of something, wages of £300 a week

words.definitionEn: to walk slowly around or to a place, often without any particular sense of purpose or direction
words.exampleEn: + adv./prep., She wandered aimlessly around the streets.

words.definitionEn: the process of making something, or of becoming, warm or warmer
words.exampleEn: atmospheric warming

words.definitionEn: not able to see
words.exampleEn: Doctors think he will go blind.

words.definitionEn: very far; by a large amount
words.exampleEn: She finished the race way ahead of the other runners.

words.definitionEn: lack of physical strength
words.exampleEn: The disease causes progressive muscle weakness.

words.definitionEn: a large amount of money, property, etc. that a person or country owns
words.exampleEn: the desire to gain wealth and power

words.definitionEn: having a lot of money, possessions, etc.
words.exampleEn: a wealthy businessman/individual/family

words.definitionEn: happening, done or published once a week or every week
words.exampleEn: weekly meetings

words.definitionEn: very strange or unusual and difficult to explain
words.exampleEn: I had a really weird dream last night.

words.definitionEn: the general health, happiness and safety of a person, an animal or a group
words.exampleEn: We are concerned about the child's welfare.

words.definitionEn: a chemical, usually a liquid, that contains hydrogen and has a pH of less than seven. The hydrogen can be replaced by a metal to form a salt. Acids usually have a bitter sharp taste and can often burn holes in or damage things they touch.
words.exampleEn: The acid burned a hole in her coat.

words.definitionEn: a plant grown for its grain that is used to produce the flour for bread, cakes, pasta, etc.; the grain of this plant
words.exampleEn: wheat flour

words.definitionEn: a hard hit with the hand, a weapon, etc.
words.exampleEn: She received a severe blow on the head.

words.definitionEn: used to compare or contrast two facts
words.exampleEn: Some of the studies show positive results, whereas others do not.

words.definitionEn: in any place
words.exampleEn: Sit wherever you like.

words.definitionEn: a low, quiet voice or the sound it makes
words.exampleEn: in a whisper, ‘I love you,’ he said in a whisper.

words.definitionEn: to speak very quietly to somebody so that other people cannot hear what you are saying
words.exampleEn: Don't you know it's rude to whisper?

words.definitionEn: the person or people who; any person who
words.exampleEn: Whoever says that is a liar.

words.definitionEn: used instead of ‘who’ as the object of a verb or preposition
words.exampleEn: Whom did they invite?

words.definitionEn: by a lot of people; in or to many places
words.exampleEn: The term is widely used in everyday speech.

words.definitionEn: existing or happening over a large area or among many people
words.exampleEn: The storm caused widespread damage.

words.definitionEn: animals, birds, insects, etc. that are wild and live in a natural environment
words.exampleEn: policies designed to protect wildlife

words.definitionEn: not objecting to doing something; having no reason for not doing something
words.exampleEn: Many consumers are willing to pay more for organic food

words.definitionEn: to have many bends and twists
words.exampleEn: wind + adv./prep., The path wound down to the beach.

words.definitionEn: metal in the form of thin thread; a piece of this
words.exampleEn: a coil of copper wire

words.definitionEn: the ability to make sensible decisions and give good advice because of the experience and knowledge that you have
words.exampleEn: She was known to be a woman of great wisdom.

words.definitionEn: able to make sensible decisions and give good advice because of the experience and knowledge that you have
words.exampleEn: a wise man

words.definitionEn: to take money out of a bank account
words.exampleEn: withdraw something, With this account, you can withdraw up to £300 a day.

words.definitionEn: a person who sees something happen and is able to describe it to other people
words.exampleEn: He failed to interview a key witness.

words.definitionEn: to see something happen (typically a crime or an accident)
words.exampleEn: to witness an accident/a murder/an attack

words.definitionEn: all the people who work for a particular company, organization, etc.
words.exampleEn: The factory has a 1 000-strong workforce.

words.definitionEn: the office, factory, etc. where people work
words.exampleEn: the introduction of new technology into the workplace

words.definitionEn: a period of discussion and practical work on a particular subject, in which a group of people share their knowledge and experience
words.exampleEn: There will be the opportunity for practical theatre work in drama workshops.

words.definitionEn: a long, thin creature with a soft body and no bones or legs
words.exampleEn: birds looking for worms

words.definitionEn: more problems or bad news
words.exampleEn: I'm afraid there is worse to come.

words.definitionEn: to accept that something is true
words.exampleEn: acknowledge something, She refuses to acknowledge the need for reform.

words.definitionEn: the most serious or unpleasant thing that could happen; the part, situation, possibility, etc. that is worse than any other
words.exampleEn: When they did not hear from her, they feared the worst.

words.definitionEn: an amount of something that has the value mentioned
words.exampleEn: The winner will receive fifty pounds' worth of books.

words.definitionEn: an injury to part of the body, especially one in which a hole is made in the skin using a weapon
words.exampleEn: a gunshot/stab wound

words.definitionEn: to injure part of the body, especially by making a hole in the skin using a weapon
words.exampleEn: He had been wounded in the arm.

words.definitionEn: brave and confident; not afraid to say what you feel or to take risks
words.exampleEn: It was a bold move on their part to open a business in France.

words.definitionEn: to cover something completely in paper or other material, for example when you are giving it as a present
words.exampleEn: wrap something up, He spent the evening wrapping up the Christmas presents.

words.definitionEn: the joint between the hand and the arm
words.exampleEn: She's broken her wrist.

words.definitionEn: behaviour that is not honest or morally acceptable
words.exampleEn: Children must be taught the difference between right and wrong.

words.definitionEn: despite what has just been said
words.exampleEn: It's a small car, yet it's surprisingly spacious.

words.definitionEn: an occasion when a bomb is dropped or left somewhere; the act of doing this
words.exampleEn: recent bombings in major cities

words.definitionEn: an area or a region with a particular feature or use
words.exampleEn: a war/combat/demilitarized/exclusion zone

words.definitionEn: something that forms a connection between people or groups, such as a feeling of friendship or shared ideas and experiences
words.exampleEn: the bond that links us

words.definitionEn: to gain something by your own efforts, ability or behaviour
words.exampleEn: She has acquired a good knowledge of English.

words.definitionEn: an arrangement that you make in advance to buy a ticket to travel somewhere, go to the theatre, etc.
words.exampleEn: a booking form/hall/clerk

words.definitionEn: something that helps or encourages somebody/something
words.exampleEn: a great/tremendous/welcome boost

words.definitionEn: to make something increase, or become better or more successful
words.exampleEn: to boost exports/profits

words.definitionEn: to share a border with another country or area
words.exampleEn: the countries bordering the Baltic

words.definitionEn: certain or likely to happen, or to do or be something
words.exampleEn: There are bound to be changes when the new system is introduced.

words.definitionEn: either of the two round soft parts at the front of a woman’s body that produce milk when she has had a baby
words.exampleEn: She put the baby to her breast.

words.definitionEn: baked clay used for building walls, houses and other buildings; an individual block of this
words.exampleEn: The school is built of brick.

words.definitionEn: lasting only a short time; short
words.exampleEn: a brief visit/meeting/conversation

words.definitionEn: for a short time
words.exampleEn: He had spoken to Emma only briefly.

words.definitionEn: wide
words.exampleEn: a broad street/avenue/river

words.definitionEn: a radio or television programme
words.exampleEn: (British English), a party political broadcast (= for example, before an election)

words.definitionEn: to send out programmes on television or radio
words.exampleEn: The concert will be broadcast live (= at the same time as it takes place) tomorrow evening.

words.definitionEn: a person whose job is presenting or talking on television or radio programmes
words.exampleEn: She is a writer and broadcaster on environmental matters.

words.definitionEn: generally, without considering details
words.exampleEn: Broadly speaking, I agree with you.

words.definitionEn: the money that is available to a person or an organization and a plan of how it will be spent over a period of time
words.exampleEn: an annual budget of £10 million

words.definitionEn: any small insect
words.exampleEn: There's a bug crawling up your arm.

words.definitionEn: a small metal object that is fired from a gun
words.exampleEn: He was found to have a single bullet wound in his chest.

words.definitionEn: a number of things of the same type which are growing or fastened together
words.exampleEn: a bunch of bananas, grapes, etc.

words.definitionEn: to make something such as a device or chemical process start working
words.exampleEn: The burglar alarm is activated by movement.

words.definitionEn: an injury or a mark caused by fire, heat or acid
words.exampleEn: to have minor/severe/third-degree burns

words.definitionEn: a plant that grows thickly with several hard stems coming up from the root
words.exampleEn: a rose/holly bush
